spec_match.rationale: Concepts exercise the exact current W3C surface: both requestPermission() statics called on the interface objects (never instances), gesture-gated subscribe flows with honest no-gate legacy fallback (request-orientation, request-motion), and the cross-browser branch pattern (feature-detect-gate). Resolved values are only 'granted'/'denied' everywhere; 'prompt' is correctly treated as internal permission state that drives the NotAllowedError rejection when transient activation is missing (spec method steps, verified).

interactive.rationale: Real controls drive real behavior: enable buttons call the actual statics from user gestures, live readouts/visualizations activate only on real (non-null) sensor samples, manual previews are labelled 'SIMULATION — not sensor data' and never write to the real readouts, and the shake threshold/magnitude sliders exercise the detection logic without faking sensor input.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index lede and cards match the delivered concepts and the current sources: two static methods, 'granted'/'denied' resolution, and the directly sourced statement that WebKit currently ships the behavior on iOS (without asserting an unsupported exact version); enabling-it now provenances the rollout (v150 dev trial listing; v151 listing Enabled by default; detail desktop 151 'In development'/'Prepare to ship', API updated 2026-07-15) and names the NotAllowedError consequence.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the honest negative branches: gate-absent legacy attach (no requestPermission), unsupported event interface, null-axis events on sensor-less devices with a 2.5s no-data timeout — and this cycle made the no-data message gate-aware (the no-gate path no longer claims 'Permission granted' when nothing was requested).

references.rationale: Index cites chromestatus.com/feature/5915984063889408, the W3C spec (+ TR requestPermission anchor), the w3c/deviceorientation#57 proposal, and the exact MDN method page (requestPermission_static, verified 200 with Specifications + compat on 2026-07-24).

conformance.rationale: New immutable root suite (this cycle, 7 assertions): both event interfaces, both statics' typeof on the interface objects, the [SecureContext] gate, statics-not-on-instances binding evidence, and zero-required-argument signature evidence — all checked WITHOUT invoking requestPermission (no permission is auto-requested, per the no-auto-permission rule). Anchors verified present in the spec DOM. Executes live at /v150/deviceorientation-events-permission-request-api/conformance/.

runtime_evidence.rationale: chrome-devtools-mcp on desktop Chrome stable (2026-07-24): all visible controls exercised at 1280×800 and 390×844 — feature-detect re-run (events present, both gates absent, verdict + snippet correct), orientation enable click (legacy no-gate attach + honest no-data/sensor timeout), motion enable click + threshold/simulation sliders, zero console errors and zero failed requests. No permission API was auto-invoked.

telemetry_health.rationale: Concepts emit showcaseTelemetry.assert events per branch (gate-probe ran/consistent, permission granted/honoured, data-received, no-sensor-labelled, event-supported); telemetry POSTs returned 202 during validation.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, button background+color pairs, focus-visible styles, no static-card pattern; this cycle's edits were copy/attribute level and preserve layout.

accessibility.rationale: role=status/aria-live on status lines and readouts, labelled sliders, semantic probe table with aria-label, disabled-button states exposed; the permission pill state is text, not color-only.

mobile_desktop_parity.rationale: Matrix pass after in-place fixes found by this cycle's 390px validation: all four pages overflowed via unbreakable identifiers (DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission() tokens on index + both request pages; the probe table's long surface names on feature-detect-gate at 519px) — fixed with page-local code{overflow-wrap:anywhere} on three pages and overflow-wrap on probe-table cells; re-verified all four scrollWidth equals viewport at 1280×800 and 390×844 with every control exercised (enable button 45px measured; stage grid collapses to one column ≤560px as designed).

openQuestions.title: Re-verify rollout copy and gate behavior when Chrome 151 reaches stable
openQuestions.detail: Copy is provenanced to the 2026-07-24 fetch of the direct chromestatus API (updated 2026-07-15: v150 dev trial listing, v151 listing Enabled by default, detail desktop 151 'In development' with active 'Prepare to ship' stage). Once 151 ships, the gate should appear on stable Chrome without a flag — re-run the feature-detect gate and the immutable suite there, and update the enabling-it copy.

guidanceConsulted.query: permission request button flow with honest unavailable-sensor fallback states
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Provide clear reason and context BEFORE requesting powerful browser permissions; do not request consent automatically or deceptively.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Verified applied: each request page presents purpose copy and an explicit user-initiated Enable button (transient activation) before any requestPermission() call; no page auto-invokes the permission algorithm, and the immutable suite deliberately avoids invoking it too (signature/binding checks only).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: chrome-devtools-mcp flows at 1280×800 and 390×844; suite assertion review (no call sites)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region announcements for dynamic probe/status results after button activation
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic state through live regions tied to the activating control; keep semantic tables and named controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Verified applied across all three concepts (status lines + readouts with aria-live observed in the a11y tree; permission pill as text state; labeled sliders).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: chrome-devtools-mcp a11y-tree snapshots on all four pages, 2026-07-24

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form controls selects checkboxes and labeled inputs in interactive demo pages
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate labels with every input and expose state changes programmatically.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Verified applied: threshold/magnitude/beta/gamma sliders are label-wrapped or for/id-associated with text output of current values.

summary: Source-review of the DeviceOrientation permission family: the existing pages were already correct on the two highest-risk points (statics called on the interface objects; promises resolving only 'granted'/'denied'). This cycle hardened precision: enabling-it now provenances the rollout (v150 dev trial listing; v151 Enabled-by-default listing; detail desktop 151 'In development'/'Prepare to ship', API updated 2026-07-15) and names the NotAllowedError consequence of missing transient activation (verified in the spec method steps); the orientation readout now labels each sample from event.absolute rather than assuming every ordinary deviceorientation sample is relative, and a new note explains absolute=true semantics (Earth reference frame via magnetometer and deviceorientationabsolute; the spec marks permission integration and the absolute event at risk); the no-data timeout message is now gate-aware on both request pages. Added the immutable root suite (7 assertions; binding/signature-level only — no permission auto-request). Missing-suite denominator 13→12 expected (786/798).
